phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your ball-control auto harass beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — ball-control auto harass edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your ball-control auto harass beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — ball-control auto harass edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your ball-control auto harass on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your ball-control auto harass in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight deficit at two-plus items: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your ball-control auto harass on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Ball on the wave, autos on his face — your passive-stacked autos win level 1 against almost every mid. Place, don't throw.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q-W online: the classic chunk combo. Trade it on his last-hits and keep the ball where his approach must cross it.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the kit: ball-on-self beats his engage, ball-on-wave farms, ball-on-him threatens. The chess match begins.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your ball-control auto harass out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your ball-control auto harass beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.why: Late Ori is the teamfight: protect-ball on your diver, shockwave their clump, and dissonance the choke they retreat through.

phases.why: Shockwave online — your lane presence doubles without casting anything. Every wave he contests is a potential flip.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: Q-W chunks a third of his bar. Shove, rotate, and let the ball-delivery threat run the river fights.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Ori is the teamfight: protect-ball on your diver, shockwave their clump, and dissonance the choke they retreat through.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Ball on the wave, autos on his face — your passive-stacked autos win level 1 against almost every mid. Place, don't throw.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q-W online: the classic chunk combo. Trade it on his last-hits and keep the ball where his approach must cross it.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the kit: ball-on-self beats his engage, ball-on-wave farms, ball-on-him threatens. The chess match begins.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — Q-W every wave contact now. The Dissonance field controls every step he takes near the ball.

phases.why: Shockwave online — your lane presence doubles without casting anything. Every wave he contests is a potential flip.
